meta.title: Japan Financial Conditions Tracker
meta.framework: Bank of Japan Review 2026-E-4 (two-stage assessment of financial conditions)
meta.data_mode: LIVE (official sources) + SEED fallback for un-fetched series
meta.source_note: Seed values are illustrative approximations anchored to recent observations; run etl/fetch.py for authoritative data from BoJ/MoF/e-Stat/FRED.
meta.score_window: Most axes are z-scored over Jan 2005–present (monthly). The headline real-rate axis is scored over its LONGEST available history on an ex-post basis (nominal − realized core CPI; 1Y/3Y from 1974, 10Y from 1986); the 2005+ ex-ante read is shown as a reference. See the real-rate horizon table.

headline.assessment: The composite Financial Conditions Index reads +0.82 (accommodative relative to its 2005+ history), indicating that financial conditions remain accommodative even after the policy rate was raised to 1.00%. The real policy rate is about -0.98%, roughly 0.62pp below the midpoint natural-rate estimate (-0.36%), so on the natural-rate lens the stance is still accommodative; the six-model natural-rate band (-0.94% to +0.53%) means this read carries wide uncertainty. Horizon: the headline real-rate axis is now scored over its longest available history (ex-post, since 1974/1986), not the ZIRP/NIRP-dominated 2005+ window (kept as a reference). On that long baseline the real-rate stance is mildly accommodative; it reads broadly neutral versus 1990 and mildly restrictive only versus post-1995 baselines (see the horizon table). Other axes remain on 2005+ history.

reference.label: 2005+ ex-ante
reference.note: Reference only — biased toward 'restrictive'. Its 2005–present baseline is dominated by zero/negative interest-rate policy, so the average real rate is unusually low and any normalization scores above that depressed mean.

policy_rate.name: Uncollateralized Overnight Call Rate (policy rate)
policy_rate.notes: BoJ target policy rate. Hiked to 0.50 Jan2025; 0.75 Dec2025; 1.00 Jun2026.

jgb_1y.name: JGB Yield 1-Year
jgb_1y.notes: MoF reference interest rates (daily) aggregated to monthly.

infexp_1y.name: Inflation Expectations 1-Year (composite)
infexp_1y.notes: Composite index of inflation expectations (staff-style). Used to deflate nominal rates.

real_policy_rate.name: Real Policy Rate (policy rate minus 1y expected inflation)
real_policy_rate.notes: Real ex-ante policy rate. Compared with the natural rate to gauge the policy stance.

real_1y_xp.name: Real Interest Rate 1-Year (ex-post)
real_1y_xp.notes: Headline real 1y: nominal minus realized core CPI. Scored over longest history (1974+).

real_3y_xp.name: Real Interest Rate 3-Year (ex-post)
real_3y_xp.notes: Headline real 3y (2y-5y interp minus realized core CPI). Scored over longest history (1974+).

real_10y_xp.name: Real Interest Rate 10-Year (ex-post)
real_10y_xp.notes: Headline real 10y: nominal minus realized core CPI. Scored over longest history (1986+).

lending_rate.name: Average Contracted Interest Rate on New Loans
lending_rate.notes: Average contracted rate on new loans and discounts (domestic banks).

corp_bond_spread.name: Corporate Bond Spread (AA 10y over JGB)
corp_bond_spread.notes: Issuance/secondary spread over JGB. Lower spread = more accommodative.

tankan_lend_large.name: Tankan Lending Attitude DI - Large Enterprises
tankan_lend_large.notes: DI = accommodative minus severe. Positive = accommodative.

tankan_lend_small.name: Tankan Lending Attitude DI - Small Enterprises
tankan_lend_small.notes: DI = accommodative minus severe. Positive = accommodative.

tankan_finpos_large.name: Tankan Financial Position DI - Large Enterprises
tankan_finpos_large.notes: DI = easy minus tight. Positive = easy.

tankan_finpos_small.name: Tankan Financial Position DI - Small Enterprises
tankan_finpos_small.notes: DI = easy minus tight. Positive = easy.

topix.name: TOPIX Index
topix.notes: Broad equity index. Higher = more accommodative (balance-sheet/wealth channel).

usdjpy.name: USD/JPY Exchange Rate
usdjpy.notes: Higher USDJPY = weaker yen = more accommodative for exporters/profits.

bank_lending_yoy.name: Bank Lending Outstanding (y/y %)
bank_lending_yoy.notes: Lending by domestic commercial banks (avg amounts outstanding) y/y.

cp_corpbond_yoy.name: CP and Corporate Bonds Outstanding (y/y %)
cp_corpbond_yoy.notes: CP and corporate bonds outstanding (end of period) y/y.

core_cpi_yoy.name: Core CPI (ex-fresh food) y/y
core_cpi_yoy.notes: MIC CPI core (ex-fresh food). Context for 2% target.

potential_growth.name: Potential Growth Rate
potential_growth.notes: BoJ staff estimate. Key driver of natural rate in structural models.

natural_rate_low.name: Natural Rate of Interest - Lower Bound

natural_rate_mid.name: Natural Rate of Interest - Midpoint
natural_rate_mid.notes: Midpoint of 6-model range. ~-0.2pct as of 2025Q3 (range center).

natural_rate_high.name: Natural Rate of Interest - Upper Bound
